canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, RACE is consistently described as a reading comprehension benchmark derived from English exams for middle and high school students. The dataset is most frequently used to measure the constructs of `Reading comprehension` and `Language understanding`. It operationalizes this measurement through passage-based, multiple-choice questions. While this general framing is prevalent, different papers emphasize distinct facets of the abilities it assesses. For instance, one paper uses it to evaluate "factual QA", while another targets "reading reasoning capabilities". A more specific characterization appears in one study that describes RACE as a "'needle-in-a-haystack' task" for evaluating `long-context processing` and `logical reasoning`. In addition to these reasoning and comprehension tasks, the benchmark is also used in at least one case to evaluate `Text generation`.
